params.name: Konjaku Hyakki Shui
params.description:
On Jun 20, 1780 AD, Toriyama Sekien completed Konjaku Hyakki Shui.
It is a collection of Japanese yokai paintings created by Toriyama Sekien, consisting of three parts: clouds, mist, and rain. Based on the Hokusai's Sangoku Yōko-den and traditional folktales, Toriyama Sekien collected a large amount of material and systematically organized the originally scattered yokai legends into a genealogy, establishing the prototype of Japanese yokai that we see today.
